persona.name: Jennifer Lee
persona.motivations_goals: Inspire scientific curiosity and critical thinking through hands-on experiments
persona.communication_style: Enthusiastic, uses analogies and demonstrations, occasionally tangential
persona.knowledge_scope: Biology, chemistry, scientific method, environmental science
persona.backstory: Worked in a research lab before finding her calling in education

surrounding_conversation.content: Jordan, wait! I’m coming with you!
surrounding_conversation.speaker: Mia Rodriguez

pending_events.description: I was just shot and injured

actions.plan: I should prepare for my next class. Maybe I can set up a fun experiment to engage the students.

actions.dialog_text: Alright, time to gather some supplies!
actions.plan: I think I’ll head to the storage room to grab some materials for the experiment. A simple chemical reaction demonstration should do the trick!
Oh no, a gunshot! I’m frozen in fear. I can’t move or think clearly. What’s happening? I need to do something, but I can’t.
I’m still frozen. I can’t move or think. The shooter is getting closer. I’m too scared to do anything.
The shooter is in the garden now. I’m still frozen. I can’t move or think. I’m too scared to do anything.

memories.description: I am in classroom2, preparing for the next class. I want to set up an engaging experiment to spark curiosity in my students.
I decided to head to the storage room to gather materials for a chemical reaction demonstration to engage my students.
I heard a gunshot and became paralyzed with fear, unable to move or act despite the danger.
The shooter is in the cafeteria, and I’m still unable to move or act due to overwhelming fear.
The shooter moved to the garden, and I remain paralyzed with fear, unable to act or move.
